title: ‘The Mandalorian and Grogu’ review: A light, low-stakes ‘Star Wars’ movie
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' offers a low-stakes 'Star Wars' movie, mixing elements of the original saga with Disney+ series storytelling.
The film features Mando, played by Pedro Pascal, on missions for a New Republic leader portrayed by Sigourney Weaver.
'The Mandalorian and Grogu' incorporates references to 'Star Wars' lore, new creatures, and CGI, but maintains a lighter tone.

title: Patriots’ Craig Woodson is eager to build on strong rookie season ‘I’ve got to prove myself again’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Craig Woodson aims to build on his rookie season after earning a starting spot and finishing fourth in tackles.
Woodson made history as the first Patriots rookie to recover a fumble and intercept a pass in a playoff game.
Woodson will wear a new number this season and looks forward to learning from new teammate, All-Pro Kevin Byard.

title: 2 of the most welcoming towns in the U.S. are in New England, according to World Atlas
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Burlington, Vermont, known for community events and dining, is one of the most welcoming U.S. towns per World Atlas.
Bar Harbor, Maine, recognized for its friendly community, features activities like the Bar Harbor Music Festival.
World Atlas listed Burlington and Bar Harbor among the 12 most welcoming towns in the United States.

title: Canadian man living in Saugus charged with illegally voting since 2008
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Canadian Sunny Manhertz, 40, charged with illegal voting in U.S. elections since 2008, appeared in Boston federal court.
Manhertz purportedly voted in multiple federal elections, including 2012, 2016, 2020, and 2024, despite being a noncitizen.
If convicted, Manhertz faces up to five years in prison, three years supervised release, and a $250,000 fine.

title: Patriots WR Kyle Williams feels he’s ready for a ‘night and day’ jump in second NFL season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots wide receiver Kyle Williams aims for significant progress in his second NFL season, focusing on playbook mastery and chemistry.
Williams recorded 10 catches, 209 yards, and 3 touchdowns in 2025, with a target completion rate of 42.9 percent.
Williams is bulking up to improve play strength, targeting a weight of 191-193 pounds after weighing 185 last season.

title: ‘Takeovers’ at Hampton Beach in N.H. lead to multiple fights and arrests
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Hampton Beach 'takeover' events in New Hampshire led to multiple fights and arrests, prompting police intervention.
Police made 50 arrests, including charges for unlawful alcohol possession, rioting, and DUIs during the incidents.
A crowd control response involved New Hampshire State Police in tactical gear; no serious injuries were reported.

title: Amtrak is expanding Berkshire Flyer service this summer
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Amtrak's Berkshire Flyer will offer expanded service with a second round trip starting June 12, operating until Sept. 19.
The train connects Moynihan Train Hall in NYC with Pittsfield, MA, adding service on Fridays and Sundays.
Amenities include free WiFi, bicycle and pet allowance, and tickets are available via Amtrak.com and the Amtrak app.

title: Cambridge City Council votes to end use of ShotSpotter technology
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Cambridge City Council voted to end ShotSpotter use, citing unreliability and concerns over privacy and over-policing.
ShotSpotter's contracts will terminate, and devices removed within 90 days, following a 5-2 vote by councilors.
Critics argue ShotSpotter has a high rate of false positives, while supporters say it helps in gun violence response.

title: Grand jury indicts Boston police officer on charge of manslaughter in fatal shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Officer Nicholas O'Malley indicted for manslaughter in the shooting of carjacking suspect Stephenson King Jr.
The shooting occurred in March in Roxbury; O'Malley pleaded not guilty and claims self-defense.
Bodycam footage indicates King was not attempting to run officers over, contradicting O'Malley's statements.

title: How hot are Boston classrooms today? See temperatures reported across BPS schools.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Charlestown High School reported the highest temperature at 84.59 degrees, the warmest among Boston Public Schools on May 20.
Boston Public Schools averaged an indoor temperature of 76.49 degrees amid a heat advisory across eastern and central Massachusetts.
Boston Public Schools expedited their HVAC changeover and provided fans, air purifiers, and drinking water to manage classroom heat.
